page: Microsoft Azure A4m_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Av2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. This configuration provides 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The CPU includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and a 50 MB L3 cache. It supports hardware-assisted features such as AVX, AES cryptography, BMI2, ADX, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 extensions.

The server is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 41 GB of local storage, but does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware. This hardware profile positions the instance as a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power, though it trades off raw processing speed and storage capacity.

Benchmark results place the A4m_v2 in the lower tier of performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is measured at 123.33 GB/sec for cached small blocks and drops to 12.77 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Redis SET operations achieve 1.84 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 1.45 GB/sec. Given these performance characteristics and the high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is best suited for entry-level database hosting, basic caching layers, lightweight web serving, or development and testing environments that require moderate memory capacity without intensive computational demands.
description: Microsoft Azure A4m_v2 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine from the Av2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor offering 4 dedicated physical cores at 2.3 GHz, backed by a 50 MB L3 cache. The instance features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a generous 8.0 GB of memory per core, alongside 41 GB of local storage and no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks, including stress-ng and memory bandwidth tests, place this server in the lower tier, with uncached large-block read bandwidth at 12.77 GB/sec. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-dependent workloads that do not require high compute performance, making it suitable for lightweight databases, caching layers, and development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure A4m_v2 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 41 GB of local storage, designed for entry-level memory-dependent workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure A4m_v2 is a memory-intensive VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 41 GB local storage, optimized for basic database and caching tasks.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring four dedicated cores and thirty-two gigabytes of RAM for entry-level database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
41 GB of local storage capacity with no bundled GPU
Suitable for lightweight database hosting and caching layers
Cost-efficient option for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads
